description: ECDSA-P256 golden test vector. Wire form is IEEE 1363 (r‖s) — 64 raw bytes, 88 base64 chars — NOT DER. Signature is deterministic per RFC 6979 with SHA-256 so the value is reproducible. Producer content is byte-identical to sig-001 vector 1, so implementations can compare across algorithms. Test keypair MUST NOT be used in production: the private scalar is 1 (the smallest valid P-256 scalar) and the resulting public key is the curve's generator point G. The keypair exists only to make the test vector reproducible.

test_keypair.warning: TEST ONLY. The private scalar is publicly known (= 1). The public key is the P-256 generator G. Never use this keypair for real publications.

vectors.name: Minimal first-version publish request, signed (ecdsa-p256, RFC 6979 deterministic)

producer_content.title: Golden test vector — minimal first version

expected.signature_wire_form: IEEE 1363 (r‖s) — 32 bytes r concatenated with 32 bytes s = 64 raw bytes; base64 encoded with padding = 88 chars

publish_request_body.title: Golden test vector — minimal first version

verification_steps: 1. Strip exclusion set from publish_request_body to produce producer_content.
2. JCS-canonicalize producer_content; result MUST equal expected.canonical_form.
3. SHA-256 of canonical_form (UTF-8 bytes); hex of digest with 'sha256:' prefix MUST equal expected.content_hash.

4. Sign ASCII bytes of expected.content_hash with the test private key, using RFC 6979 deterministic ECDSA over P-256 with SHA-256 as the hash. Encode the signature as IEEE 1363 r‖s (32+32 = 64 raw bytes), base64 with padding. Result MUST equal expected.signature_value_base64. Implementations using non-deterministic ECDSA MUST still produce a 64-byte r‖s output, but their value will differ; they verify via step 5 instead of byte-comparing.

5. Reconstruct the public key: build the SEC1 uncompressed point 0x04 || x || y (65 bytes) from public_key_jwk.x and public_key_jwk.y (base64url-decoded, 32 bytes each), or use public_key_uncompressed_sec1_hex directly. Verify the signature with this public key over the ASCII bytes of body.content_hash (with the 'sha256:' prefix included). Verification MUST succeed for both the deterministic value above AND any conformant implementation's own r‖s output.
6. Compute lin: + lowercase_hex(SHA-256(registry_assigned.ctx_id)); result MUST equal registry_assigned.lineage_id.

notes: ECDSA-P256 is non-deterministic by default. Implementations following this fixture's signature_value_base64 byte-for-byte MUST use RFC 6979 deterministic ECDSA (SHA-256 hash). Implementations using random-k ECDSA cannot reproduce the value but MUST still verify it (step 5) and MUST produce a 64-byte IEEE 1363 r‖s output for their own signing path. DER-encoded signatures are NON-CONFORMANT regardless of length.
